source.title: A component analysis of cognitive-behavioral treatment for depression
source.venue: Journal of Consulting and Clinical Psychology

confidence_rationale: Foundational RCT demonstrating BA alone matches full CBT efficacy. Directly supports INV-054's prediction that external scaffolding of reward contact (BA scheduling) is sufficient to restore terrain, without cognitive restructuring. Source quality high (n=150 RCT, JCCP). Mapping fidelity good: the active ingredient being reward-contact scheduling rather than cognitive change maps onto benefit_exposure as the key variable. Minor caveat: does not test the loop mechanism or attractor structure directly.
failure_signatures: Full CT did not outperform BA component alone, suggesting the attractor model may underspecify the role of cognitive content in recovery.
BA component also changed negative thinking, challenging a clean separation between behavioral (terrain) and cognitive (schema) recovery pathways.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Behavioral activation alone -- scheduling external contact with rewarding activities -- produces equivalent antidepressant outcomes to full cognitive-behavioral therapy at acute termination and 6-month follow-up.
mapping.ree_translation: INV-054 predicts that once benefit terrain collapses, internal motivation cannot self-restart without externally scaffolded benefit encounters. BA provides exactly this forcing function: it increases benefit_exposure above the SD-012 seeding threshold from outside the motivational system, enabling terrain recovery before internal VALENCE_WANTING has recovered.
mapping.mapping_caveat: BA outcome studies measure symptom change, not benefit terrain directly. The BA mechanism in REE (forcing benefit_exposure above seeding threshold) is plausible but inferred from the equivalent-outcome finding; the paper does not measure motivational trajectories. Transfer from clinical populations to REE grid-world agent also involves abstraction loss.
mapping.source_context: n=150 outpatients with major depressive disorder, randomised to BA, BA+automatic thoughts, or full CT. Naturalistic clinical setting, US.

confidence_components.notes: High-quality RCT, canonical citation in BA literature. Mapping fidelity penalised because the paper establishes BA efficacy, not the terrain-loop mechanism specifically. Transfer risk modest: human clinical data maps cleanly to the therapeutic prediction in INV-054.
